100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 9, 1926, the British Empire celebrated the coronation of King Edward VIII, marking a significant moment in royal history despite the ongoing debates about his suitability as monarch. In the United States, tensions escalated in labor disputes as strikes spread across various industries, highlighting the growing unrest among workers. Meanwhile, in Germany, the political landscape remained unstable as the Weimar Republic faced challenges from both left and right factions, raising concerns about its future.
